project_title: Algorithmic Active Measures - data poisoning as counterintelligence tradecraft in AI-mediated intelligence analysis
product_name: Algorithmic Active Measures - data poisoning as counterintelligence tradecraft in AI-mediated intelligence analysis
final_object_type: analytic method or human-AI evaluation module
runtime_target: structured public page plus project-specific data packet and proof runner
operational_end_state: A user can apply a method, compare baseline and method-supported reasoning, record human judgment, and preserve the claim boundary.
core_modules: baseline answer | structured review | human delta | confidence gate
source_strategy: Use sources to connect counterintelligence tradecraft, active measures, data poisoning, analytic deception, and human-AI review burden. The proof must show actor objective, poisoned evidence pathway, analytic uptake or rejection, and bounded warning.
source_requirement: Title-level routing can strengthen project planning and proof design. Public claims require project evidence or passage-level source confirmation.
source_gap: passage-level close reading still required before quotation, page-specific citation, or strong literature-positioning claim
test_suite: claim ceiling test: public claims cannot exceed built evidence | source test: metadata-routed sources require close reading before citation or quotation | privacy test: no local paths or private directories in public deploy | human-AI delta test: structured human review changes, narrows, blocks, or justifies output
current_build_status: source-backed proof build; needs source-to-output capture and close-read strengthening
proof_to_full_build_gap: Bind at least one close-read source to one output-changing component and capture the source-to-output trace.
first_full_build: Make the public-safe bridge from tradecraft to data poisoning explicit enough that the study's distinctive mechanism is visible.
next_implementation_steps: 1. create or confirm runtime data schema; 2. implement one end-to-end fixture; 3. capture proof artifact; 4. run source and claim-ceiling gates; 5. publish reviewer-safe demo
claim_boundary: This is a full-build plan, not a claim that the full operational system has already been completed.
